rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE ET AL VS. UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Electronic Communications; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: May 28, 2019 Line 3, PLAINTIFFS JOHN DOE, and JANE DOE's Motion To Compel Production Of Electronic Communications Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court has reviewed the parties' detailed meet and confer correspondence and will hear argument relative to the substance and process of the referenced email search and production. The Court is interested in the work-responsibility details for each custodian for which an email search is sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/JPT).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Urban Compass, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: May 28, 2019 Line 5, PLAINTIFF DPPM, INC. DBA ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE, A CALIFORNIA Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Urban Compass, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties shall address the present status of the defendant's document search and production. The defendant shall be prepared to provide a committed date for completion of production. Monetary sanctions are denied based on the recent entry of defendant's counsel who appears to have diligently undertaken fulfillment of the defendant's discovery obligations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DONALD R MCCAFFREE ET AL VS. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA INC. A NEW JERSEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Determination Of Petition For Coordination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANTs ANDERSON ENTERPRISES INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION and VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Determination Of Petition For Coordination Defendants Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. and Anderson Enterprises, Inc.' motion to stay proceedings pending determination of petition for coordination is GRANTED. This action is stayed until a ruling on the petition to coordinate is made by the Los Angeles Superior Court after the hearing on June 6, 2019. This court has the inherent power to issue such a stay. (Jordache Enters., Inc. v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 18 Cal.4th 739, 758.) Given the nearly 30 other matters involving substantially similar Lemon law issues and allegations of fraud and the voluminous discovery propounded by plaintiffs Donald R. McCaffree and Sara McCaffree, there is good cause to issue an interim stay pending a determination on the petition to coordinate. The stay will promote the interests of justice by avoiding waste of the parties' time and conserving court resources related to discovery. Plaintiffs' concerns about unnecessary delay are speculative and do not outweigh the risk of prejudice to defendants in responding to repetitive discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order and proposed judgment and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
